summary: Fair-quality audience recording captures an iconic early‑90s Dead show hailed as a top‑tier performance.
review: Listeners rave that this show epitomizes the peak of the early‑90s Grateful Dead experience, describing it as higher‑consciousness and one of the best from that era. The audience tape is fair with noticeable excess treble, but the crowd atmosphere shines through and adds to the overall excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers give 5‑star praise for the musical performance, while noting only modest recording issues (fair quality, too much treble). Weighted toward show quality, resulting in a high but not perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Too much treble; overall fair sound but strong crowd presence and ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the concert as an essential early‑90s performance with powerful energy and a seamless connection between band and audience.
